goal: Production deployment is verified and documented: Vercel cron config correct, all 12 Supabase migrations applied, RLS on every table, no secret reachable client-side, monitoring approach recorded with a stable health target.
why: At handoff the client must trust the deployment without re-auditing code. The code-level posture is already compliant; this phase makes it legible and verifiable and closes the two real gaps: no monitoring documented, and no clean health endpoint for an uptime monitor.
success_criteria: /api/health returns HTTP 200 unauthenticated (added to proxy allowlist) and reads no secret.
docs/monitoring.md records the UptimeRobot status page, the /api/health monitor target, the cron logs location, and Vercel error visibility, with no placeholders.

docs/deployment.md records the 12 migrations + per-table RLS evidence, the server-only secret posture with a re-runnable grep, the real cron config + CRON_SECRET guard citations, and the live-prod operator verification commands.
No deploy is triggered and no live --linked command output is fabricated; live-prod confirmation is captured as operator runbook steps.

tasks.title: Add /api/health route + document the monitoring approach
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health returns HTTP 200 with JSON containing ok:true and a timestamp, no authentication required.
The health route reads no environment secret and makes no DB/network call.
docs/monitoring.md records the UptimeRobot status page, an /api/health uptime monitor instruction, the Vercel cron logs location, and Vercel error visibility.
docs/monitoring.md contains no leftover authoring markers (verified by the grep in the plan Validation).
tasks.action: Create src/app/api/health/route.ts following the src/app/api/auth/me/route.ts pattern: import ok from @/lib/http, set runtime nodejs and dynamic force-dynamic, export GET returning ok({ status: 'ok', ts: new Date().toISOString() }); no auth, no process.env, no Supabase import. Add '/api/health' to the ALLOWLIST set in src/proxy.ts and to its comment block. Write docs/monitoring.md with Uptime monitoring, Cron monitoring (the two real vercel.json crons), and Error visibility sections.

tasks.title: Deployment hardening audit document
acceptance_criteria: docs/deployment.md lists all 12 migrations and states every created table has RLS enabled, with per-table file:line evidence for the 8 tables.
The doc records the server-only secret posture (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY only in src/lib/supabase/server.ts, no client.ts, no secret in any use-client component) with the re-runnable grep.
The doc records the real Vercel cron config and the CRON_SECRET bearer guard citations.

The doc has a Live production verification section with exact operator CLI commands (npx supabase migration list --linked, npx supabase db diff --linked, pg_tables rowsecurity query) presented as steps, not results.
The doc records the cron-fired-in-prod check (curl with CRON_SECRET expecting 200, or Vercel cron logs) and points to docs/monitoring.md naming /api/health.
docs/deployment.md contains no leftover authoring markers (verified by the grep in the plan Validation).
tasks.action: Write docs/deployment.md, every claim carrying file:line: Migrations & RLS (8 tables, create-table + enable-RLS lines; 0006/0012 alter-table); Secret posture (service-role server-only + grep); Cron config (two crons + CRON_SECRET guards); Live production verification (operator steps: migration list --linked, db diff --linked, pg_tables RLS query); Cron prod confirmation (curl with CRON_SECRET or Vercel logs); Monitoring (link docs/monitoring.md, /api/health). Do NOT deploy or run live commands.
